summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting and public hearing to review zoning case ZC2026005, a text amendment establishing the new M3 Limited High Impact Industrial District. The zoning staff presented a detailed overview of the M3 district's strict siting requirements, performance standards, and conditional use provisions designed to tightly control where and how high-impact industrial uses can occur. After public comment from one resident who spoke in favor, the board unanimously approved the text amendments and adopted resolution R-23-2026.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously (Allen, Hauserman, Hodges all yes)

Motion to approve case ZC2026005 and adopt resolution R-23-2026 establishing the M3 Limited High Impact Industrial District including exhibits A through M passed unanimously (Allen, Hauserman, Hodges all yes)
Motion to adjourn the special meeting at 6:20 p.m. passed unanimously
key_amounts: 150 acres minimum site size for M3 district
500 feet minimum site width for M3 district
40% maximum building and pavement coverage (60% must remain open space)
300 feet minimum setback from right-of-way for principal buildings
1,000 feet no-go zone around homes, town center, churches, schools, parks, and community places
50 feet minimum landscape buffer where M3 abuts another district
300 feet minimum depth/width for B2, M1, or M2 strips to count as separation buffer
notable_topics: Establishment of M3 Limited High Impact Industrial District text amendments
Strict siting requirements including acreage minimums, setbacks, and 1,000-foot no-go zones
Conditional use requirement for all major high-impact uses requiring BZA approval
Data center-specific power and water controls
Performance standards for noise, vibration, odor, dust, lighting, and hazardous materials
Closure of PD overlay and backdoor loopholes for high-impact industrial uses
Exclusionary zoning concerns explaining why outright bans are legally vulnerable
No existing M3-zoned properties in the township; any future M3 site must go through full rezoning process
attendees: Lisa (referenced by Trustee Allen, likely zoning staff)
Rick Oaks (public speaker, resident on Kazairdale Road)
notes: The M3 district was initiated by the board on March 24, 2026 via resolution R-01-2026. The Clermont County Planning Commission recommended approval on April 28, 2026, and the Goshen Township Zoning Commission recommended approval on June 2, 2026. The board emphasized this was a proactive measure to get ahead of potential high-impact industrial development before being blindsided as other townships have been. Generator testing limited to weekdays 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. with annual hour caps. Meeting lasted approximately 20 minutes (6:00-6:20 p.m.).
title: BOT Special 6-22-26
